candidate_matrix.why: Smallest mapped DB-only write: one JSON field, one Tags row update, no file system, no cascade, no bulk behavior, and easy Python-vs-Go parity fixture.
failure_cases: 400 missing JSON body or missing name

candidate_matrix.why: DB-only, but creates new identity and depends on max sort_order/default-category semantics; less minimal than tag rename for the first write.
DB-only, but optional multi-field update and category UI semantics are broader than one-field tag rename.
May migrate Notes.category_id before deleting the category and must protect default categories.
Deletes a tag and relies on Note_Tags cleanup/cascade behavior, so it has broader relational side effects than rename.
Multi-step transaction that rewrites Note_Tags and deletes source Tags.
Notes writes carry wider contract surface including tags, source URLs, note history, and delete-side file cleanup.
Likely DB-only, but nested /api/notes/... write routing would require expanding the currently hardened Go/Caddy note matcher and is not the smallest safe first write.
Broader row creation/order/batch semantics; batch delete can become file-coupled.
Filesystem-coupled and outside DB-only write scope.
Bulk/file/process/config/service side effects are explicitly outside the first Go write route.

first_write_contract_for_23_4.allowed_scope: Implement only local/copied-DB Go parity for PUT /api/tags/<tag_id> after explicit 23.4 approval.

request_schema.name: required string; trim before validation and update

transaction_semantics: Reject duplicate names before update using the Tags.name uniqueness contract.
Commit only after the update succeeds.
Rollback on validation, duplicate, missing-row, or database errors.
state_invariants: Only Tags.name may change on success.
No file paths may be read, written, renamed, or deleted.
No Caddy, systemd, frontend default, production DB, Pi deploy, or public exposure change.
csrf_and_local_only_boundary: 23.4 local/copied-DB implementation must not disable the live prism-go-readonly.service query_only guard.
Live routing remains Python-owned until a later explicit gate.
Any future live route must preserve Prism's local/trusted-network boundary and Python-compatible CSRF/origin behavior.
fixture_plan: Python baseline and Go candidate use copied DB fixtures only.
Cover missing JSON body or missing name.
Cover duplicate name 409, including case-insensitive uniqueness behavior.
Assert rollback leaves Tags unchanged on every failure.
stop_conditions: Implementation touches any route other than PUT /api/tags/<tag_id>.
Implementation needs file system side effects.
Implementation disables live query_only or changes prism-go-readonly.service.
Implementation changes Caddy, frontend default API target, production DB, Pi deployment, public exposure, or Python removal.
Python-vs-Go response or DB-state parity cannot be proven with targeted fixtures.

allowed_next_step.title: First Go write route implementation gate
allowed_next_step.scope: local/copied-DB Go parity implementation only; live routing remains blocked
